---
title: "Madagascar Influenza — Phylogenetic Analysis"
subtitle: "IQ-TREE ML trees · Monophyletic clade analysis · Tree visualizations"
author: "Camila Duitama"
date: today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    output-file: "trees.html"
    output-dir: "../docs"
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

RESULTS_DIR = Path("../results")
TREES_IMG   = Path("trees_img")   # relative to docs/ — where rendered PNGs live
TREES_DIR   = Path("../trees")

trees_sum = pd.read_csv(RESULTS_DIR / "clade_summary.tsv", sep="\t")
trees_det = pd.read_csv(RESULTS_DIR / "clade_details.tsv",  sep="\t")

PALETTE = px.colors.qualitative.Bold

def seg_type(name):
    if name.startswith("HA_"):  return "HA (surface)"
    if name.startswith("NA_"):  return "NA (surface)"
    return "Internal segment"

trees_sum["type"]    = trees_sum["tree"].apply(seg_type)
trees_det["type"]    = trees_det["tree"].apply(seg_type)
trees_sum["subtype"] = trees_sum["tree"].str.replace(r"^(HA_|NA_)", "", regex=True)
```

← [Back to main report](index.html)

---

## Study question

**Does Madagascar influenza surveillance capture unique African genetic diversity?**
Or do Malagasy viruses simply mirror the continental gene pool, making in-country
sequencing epidemiologically redundant?

To answer this, we need to know whether Madagascar sequences form **monophyletic clades**
(groups sharing a common ancestor exclusive to Madagascar — evidence of locally-sustained
lineages) or are **singletons** scattered within an otherwise African tree (evidence of
sporadic importation with no local persistence).

---

## Pipeline

### Tree design

**16 maximum-likelihood trees** (IQ-TREE 3.1.0, HKY+G substitution model, seed 42)
were built across all segment × subtype combinations with ≥ 10 Madagascar and ≥ 20 African sequences.

| Category | Segments | Strategy | Trees |
|----------|----------|----------|-------|
| HA per-subtype | HA | One tree per HA subtype | 5 |
| NA per-subtype | NA | One tree per NA subtype | 5 |
| Internal merged | PB2, PB1, PA, NP, MP, NS | All subtypes merged per segment | 6 |
| **Total** | | | **16** |

**Why merge internal segments across subtypes?** Internal genes (PB2, PB1, PA, NP, MP, NS)
do not cluster by HxNy subtype — they reassort freely. Merging recovers the full
evolutionary history of each internal gene, including reassortment signatures.

**Why keep HA and NA per-subtype?** HA and NA subtypes represent distinct antigenic lineages
separated by very long branches. Mixing them produces an unresolvable star topology.

### Subsampling design

| Input | Strategy |
|-------|----------|
| Madagascar sequences | All kept |
| Africa (non-Madagascar) | Proportionally sampled by country to **4 × n_Madagascar** |

The 4× ratio ensures the tree reflects Africa's geographic diversity without being
overwhelmed by African bulk. Trees were **midpoint-rooted** before clade detection.

### Clade detection (`scripts/analyse_trees.py`)

A **monophyletic Madagascar clade** is a maximal subtree (≥ 2 tips) whose entire leaf set
consists only of Madagascar sequences. Maximal = the clade's parent still contains
non-Madagascar leaves (it is not engulfed by a larger all-Madagascar clade).

Two metrics per clade:

- **`internal_bl`** — sum of branch lengths within the clade: measures internal diversity
  (how much evolution happened inside Madagascar)
- **`stem_bl`** — the branch from the clade root to its parent node: measures divergence
  from the nearest African ancestor (how isolated the lineage became)

### Tree inventory

```{python}
#| label: tbl-tree-inventory

# Read IQ-TREE log sizes to get actual input counts per tree
display_cols = {
    "tree":              "Tree",
    "n_mdg_tips":        "Mdg tips",
    "n_africa_tips":     "Africa tips",
    "n_mdg_clades":      "Clades",
    "n_mdg_singletons":  "Singletons",
    "pct_mdg_in_clades": "% Mdg in clades",
    "mean_clade_size":   "Mean clade size",
    "mean_stem_bl":      "Mean stem BL",
}
trees_sum.rename(columns=display_cols)[list(display_cols.values())].to_html(
    index=False, classes="table table-sm table-striped table-bordered", border=0
)
```

---

## Clade analysis results

### Clades vs. singletons per tree

High clade count + many singletons → repeated short-lived importations.
Few large clades → sustained local transmission after founder event(s).

```{python}
#| label: fig-clades-bar

bar_df = trees_sum[["tree", "n_mdg_clades", "n_mdg_singletons", "type"]].copy()
bar_df = bar_df.sort_values(["type", "tree"])
bar_long = bar_df.melt(
    id_vars=["tree", "type"],
    value_vars=["n_mdg_clades", "n_mdg_singletons"],
    var_name="Category", value_name="Count",
)
bar_long["Category"] = bar_long["Category"].map(
    {"n_mdg_clades": "Clades (≥2 tips)", "n_mdg_singletons": "Singletons"}
)

fig = px.bar(
    bar_long,
    x="Count", y="tree",
    color="Category",
    facet_col="type",
    orientation="h",
    barmode="stack",
    color_discrete_map={"Clades (≥2 tips)": PALETTE[2], "Singletons": PALETTE[5]},
    labels={"tree": "Tree", "Count": "Madagascar sequences", "Category": ""},
    title="Monophyletic Madagascar clades vs. singletons per tree",
    category_orders={"type": ["HA (surface)", "NA (surface)", "Internal segment"]},
)
fig.update_layout(height=520)
fig.for_each_annotation(lambda a: a.update(text=a.text.split("=")[-1]))
fig.show()
```

### Stem branch length vs. clade size

The **stem branch length** measures how far Madagascar sequences have diverged from their
nearest African relative. High stem BL + large clades = long isolated local circulation.
Low stem BL + many small clades = recent frequent importations.

```{python}
#| label: fig-scatter

fig = px.scatter(
    trees_sum,
    x="mean_stem_bl",
    y="mean_clade_size",
    size="n_mdg_clades",
    color="type",
    text="tree",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    labels={
        "mean_stem_bl":    "Mean stem branch length",
        "mean_clade_size": "Mean clade size (tips)",
        "n_mdg_clades":    "Number of clades",
        "type":            "Segment type",
    },
    title="Stem BL vs. clade size — bubble size = number of clades",
    size_max=30,
)
fig.update_traces(textposition="top center", textfont_size=9)
fig.update_layout(height=520)
fig.show()
```

### Clade size distribution across trees

```{python}
#| label: fig-clade-sizes

fig = px.box(
    trees_det,
    x="tree", y="n_tips",
    color="type",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    points="outliers",
    labels={"n_tips": "Clade size (tips)", "tree": "Tree", "type": "Segment type"},
    title="Distribution of Madagascar clade sizes across all 16 trees",
    category_orders={"type": ["HA (surface)", "NA (surface)", "Internal segment"]},
)
fig.update_layout(xaxis_tickangle=-40, height=480)
fig.show()
```

### Internal branch length vs. stem branch length per clade

Each dot is a single clade. High `internal_bl` = diverse sequences inside Madagascar.
High `stem_bl` = large divergence from African ancestor.

```{python}
#| label: fig-clade-scatter

fig = px.scatter(
    trees_det,
    x="stem_bl",
    y="internal_bl",
    size="n_tips",
    color="type",
    hover_data=["tree", "clade_id", "n_tips", "mean_tip_dist"],
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    labels={
        "stem_bl":     "Stem branch length (divergence from Africa)",
        "internal_bl": "Internal branch length (within-clade diversity)",
        "n_tips":      "Clade size",
        "type":        "Segment type",
    },
    title="Within-clade diversity vs. divergence from Africa — per clade",
    size_max=25,
    opacity=0.7,
)
fig.update_layout(height=520)
fig.show()
```

### Interpretation

The stem BL vs. clade size scatter separates two biological regimes:

- **H6N2 and H9N2** *(top-right)*: 1–2 very large clades, `mean_stem_bl` 0.017–0.031 — an
  order of magnitude higher than seasonal subtypes. These avian subtypes entered Madagascar
  in **one or few founder events** and circulated locally long enough to accumulate
  substantial divergence from the continental gene pool. Madagascar H6N2 and H9N2 viruses
  are **genuinely distinct** and would not be recovered by any other country's surveillance.

- **Seasonal subtypes** (H1N1, H3N2, Flu B) *(bottom-left)*: 31–43 small clades,
  `mean_stem_bl` 0.001–0.002. Consistent with **annual reintroduction** from the global
  pool, each seeding a short-lived local chain. Surveillance still detects these
  importations in real time, but the sequences are not unique to Madagascar.

- **Internal segments** *(middle)*: 51–71 clades driven by reassortment — the same segment
  can arrive in different genetic backgrounds. The fragmented landscape is expected and
  not interpretable as independent introductions.

**Conclusion:** Madagascar influenza surveillance is **justified** from a genomic-epidemiology
perspective. Avian lineages (H6N2, H9N2) show founder-effect local divergence. Seasonal
human viruses show importation + local amplification patterns that would be invisible
without in-country sequencing.

---

## Tree visualizations

Trees were rendered with **ETE3** (`scripts/render_trees.py`).
Each image shows Madagascar tips in **red**, African tips in **grey**,
and **blue boxes** around detected monophyletic clades annotated with
clade size (`n=`) and stem branch length (`s=`).

Run to generate/refresh PNGs:

```bash
# On a headless server (HPC), wrap with xvfb-run:
xvfb-run -a mamba run -p ./env python scripts/render_trees.py --force
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-trees-gallery
#| output: asis

from IPython.display import display, HTML
img_dir = Path("trees_img")
treefiles = sorted(Path("../trees").glob("*.treefile"))

chunks = []
for tf in treefiles:
    img_path = img_dir / f"{tf.stem}.png"
    if img_path.exists():
        chunks.append(
            f'<div style="margin-bottom:2em">'
            f'<h3 style="font-size:1em;margin-bottom:4px">{tf.stem}</h3>'
            f'<img src="{img_path}" style="max-width:100%;border:1px solid #ddd" '
            f'alt="{tf.stem} tree"/></div>'
        )
    else:
        row = trees_sum[trees_sum["tree"] == tf.stem]
        clades = int(row["n_mdg_clades"].iloc[0]) if len(row) else "?"
        mdg    = int(row["n_mdg_tips"].iloc[0])   if len(row) else "?"
        afr    = int(row["n_africa_tips"].iloc[0]) if len(row) else "?"
        chunks.append(
            f'<div style="margin-bottom:2em;padding:1em;background:#f8f9fa;'
            f'border:1px dashed #aaa">'
            f'<strong>{tf.stem}</strong> — PNG not yet generated<br>'
            f'<em>{mdg} Mdg tips · {afr} Africa tips · {clades} clades</em><br>'
            f'<code>xvfb-run -a mamba run -p ./env python scripts/render_trees.py --force</code>'
            f'</div>'
        )

display(HTML("\n".join(chunks)))
```
